title: Miro Yamamoto
compiled_truth:
Miro Yamamoto is the founder and CEO of [Lattice Data](companies/lattice-data), a data infrastructure company he started in early 2026 after stints at two mid-stage infrastructure and developer-tools startups. People who've worked with him describe a particular combination of storytelling instinct and systems-level patience — someone who can hold an audience at a conference keynote but also sit quietly through a three-hour architecture review without fidgeting. He's fond of saying that "the best infrastructure is the kind nobody talks about," which is a little ironic given how much he himself talks about it.

Before founding Lattice Data, Miro spent roughly five years bouncing between backend infrastructure roles and developer experience teams. He was never the loudest engineer in the room, but he had a knack for framing problems in ways that made executives pay attention. That skill eventually pushed him toward starting his own thing. [Lattice Data](companies/lattice-data) was incorporated in January 2026 with a small pre-seed and a thesis around making data pipelines more predictable — less glue, fewer failure modes, tighter guarantees at the transport layer.

Yamamoto is a long-term thinker almost to a fault. Internal planning docs from Lattice's early months reportedly sketch out product roadmaps through 2030, which some advisors have gently suggested might be premature for a company that hadn't yet shipped its first SDK. Still, that forward-looking disposition has attracted a certain type of engineer to the team — people who want to build things that last rather than ship fast and patch later.

He's known in the broader infrastructure community as someone who gives unusally candid talks. At a 2027 developer event he openly discussed a failed integration that cost Lattice a potential design partner, which earned him both respect and a few raised eyebrows from investors. His relationship with the Anchor Codec ecosystem appears to be one of mutual curiosity — Lattice's work on pipeline determinism sits adjacent to some of the same problems Anchor is tackling, and there's been at least one recorded conversation between the two teams about potential overlap.

Miro lives in the Pacific Northwest, is a recreational climber, and occasionally posts long essays on infrastructure philosophy that circulate in niche engineering forums.
timeline:
- **2026-01-12** | Miro Yamamoto incorporates [Lattice Data](companies/lattice-data) with two co-founders and a small pre-seed round.
- **2026-04-03** | First engineering hire joins Lattice; Yamamoto tweets about "building the team that builds the thing."
- **2026-09-18** | Miro gives a talk at a regional infrastructure meetup on pipeline determinism; draws interest from several early design partners.
- **2027-01-27** | [Lattice Data](companies/lattice-data) closes a seed round; Yamamoto begins hiring more aggressively on the systems side.
- **2027-05-11** | Miro publishes a long-form essay on "Why Data Infrastructure Keeps Breaking" — it circulates widely in engineering Slack groups.
- **2027-07-22** | Informal conversation between Yamamoto and members of the Anchor Codec team about shared interests in deterministic primitives.
- **2027-10-04** | Gives a candid conference talk about a failed design-partner integration; the talk is later referenced in multiple infrastructure newsletters.
- **2028-02-15** | Lattice ships its first public SDK; Miro describes the launch as "embarrassingly late and exactly on time."
- **2028-06-09** | Yamamoto spotted at an invite-only infrastructure summit, reportedly exploring interoperability angles with adjacent projects including Anchor Codec.

_facts.name: Miro Yamamoto
_facts.title: Founder & CEO, Lattice Data
_facts.background: Founded Lattice Data in 2026; previously at infrastructure and developer-tools companies
